QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_21

question.text: What happens when a merchant banker fails to meet the capital adequacy requirements under Regulation 7?

answer.text: The MB is given 12 months to restore capital adequacy
The MB cannot undertake any fresh permitted activity until it becomes compliant
The MB is downgraded from Category I to Category II automatically

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When a merchant banker fails to meet capital adequacy requirements, it cannot undertake any fresh permitted activity until it restores compliance. This is a prospective restriction — ongoing mandates may continue but no new business can be taken on. **The Trap:** Automatic cancellation sounds severe but is not the immediate consequence — SEBI first restricts new activity. Automatic downgrading is also not provided for in the regulations. **Cross-Exam Context:** Capital adequacy compliance consequences are tested in NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) for broker-dealer requirements and NISM Series V-A for AMC capital requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Monitor your capital adequacy ratios monthly — falling below the threshold even briefly can cost you lucrative mandates.
